newsSinceJun6: Jun 8: shares surged ~5.6-7% to a record ~$674B European market cap after CEO Fouquet confirmed direct talks with Elon Musk about supplying EUV equipment for the SpaceX/Tesla 'Terafab' Texas fab ($55B+, reports up to $119B); Musk joined ASML's Jun 9-10 private technology conference virtually. Coverage also flags raised 2026 revenue guidance (EUR 36-40B) and a >$7B multi-year SK Hynix EUV order for HBM expansion. No signed Terafab equipment orders yet. Jun 9 ADR -0.55% as the pop digests.

sources.label: Bloomberg - Musk to address ASML event on Terafab (Jun 6, 2026)
TechTimes - ASML hits $674B Europe record on Musk Terafab bet (Jun 8, 2026)
TradingKey - ASML moved up 7.15% on Jun 8: key drivers
Simply Wall St - ASML up 8.7% after Musk Terafab tie-up and higher 2026 guidance

verdicts.rankRationale: This page already caps ASML for crowding and valuation discovery; a ~7% surge to a record $674B market cap on a Musk Terafab headline, without signed orders, is exactly the post-rerating chase the alpha lens penalizes.
verdicts.bull: Terafab would add an entirely new leading-edge customer on top of raised EUR 36-40B 2026 guidance and a >$7B SK Hynix EUV order; the monopoly is tightening, not loosening.
verdicts.neutral: Genuinely positive demand signals, but talks are not orders and ASML's bottleneck status was already fully discovered; the news mostly validates consensus.
verdicts.bear: At record market cap and +49% YTD, a speculative Musk headline pricing in unbooked demand leaves the name vulnerable to a 'talks fizzle' reversal and to China/capex-cycle disappointments.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Signed Terafab EUV/litho purchase agreements or Q2 bookings confirming the demand step-up
verdicts.primaryRisk: Terafab talks fading or being deferred, deflating a headline-driven rerating with no order backlog behind it

verdicts.rankRationale: The choke-point centrality thesis is intact and arguably strengthened by the SK Hynix EUV order, but nothing in the Terafab news changes ASML's indirect CPO linkage; already ranked deep at 72, the headline rerating only trims residual alpha at the margin.
verdicts.bull: More EUV-intensive logic and HBM capacity (Terafab, SK Hynix) raises advanced-node and packaging flows that ultimately pull CPO-adjacent demand through ASML's tools.
verdicts.neutral: News is about lithography demand broadly, not co-packaged optics specifically; the page-relevant exposure is unchanged.
verdicts.bear: A record-valuation mega-cap with indirect CPO exposure offers little asymmetric upside on this list, and China restrictions remain a live capex risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Advanced-node and packaging capacity announcements that explicitly expand CPO-relevant fab flows
verdicts.primaryRisk: China export-control escalation or a capex-cycle rollover hitting bookings

verdicts.rankRationale: The page's audit note rewards scarce bottleneck exposure that has NOT already rerated; a record market cap print on an unsigned Musk Terafab headline plus +49% YTD reduces the residual alpha this broad pick-and-shovel list is scoring.
verdicts.bull: Raised EUR 36-40B 2026 guidance, exploding EUV bookings, and a potential brand-new US hyperscale-fab customer extend the upstream demand runway into 2027-2028.
verdicts.neutral: Strong fundamentals, but they are the most widely understood fundamentals in semis; the marginal buyer is chasing headlines, not discovering the bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: If Terafab equipment orders never materialize, the Jun 8 surge unwinds and the stock re-anchors to a capex cycle where China revenue is already shrinking.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Conversion of Terafab talks into firm system orders or another guidance raise at Q2 results
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded mega-cap positioning unwinding on any bookings or guidance wobble after the record-high rerating
